ground_truth.description: Clearly a photo of Holmenkollen ski jump. Should be obvious with and without metadata due to unique architecture. No snow might throw it off.
ground_truth.photographer_description: Clearly a photo of Holmenkollen ski jump. Should be obvious with and without metadata due to its unique architecture/style. No snow might throw it off though. Daytime, autumn.
ground_truth.notes: Daytime, autumn. No snow might be confusing.

details.description: Wide view of a major ski-jump facility shot from the forecourt at its base. The structure features a long in-run ramp rising to a dramatic cantilevered tower clad in perforated/mesh steel, creating a lightweight lattice silhouette. Broad concrete spectator grandstands flank the outrun, with stairways and railings visible. Tall floodlight masts stand around the arena. Coniferous forest lines the hillside behind the venue. The sky is overcast and the ground in the plaza is gravel; a few people walk in the foreground. The form, materials, and setting strongly indicate the Holmenkollen ski-jump complex.

details.description: A large ski jumping hill, Holmenkollen, dominates the scene with a white steel ramp and elevated viewing platforms. The steep hill is flanked by wide concrete staircases on either side and a curved, lattice-like structure overhead. The foreground is a flat, gravel plaza with two people walking on the right. Surrounding the jump are dense evergreen trees, and the sky is overcast, giving a muted, cool tone. The overall setting is a sports venue in a wooded hillside, typical of the Holmenkollen area in Oslo, Norway.

entities.name: Holmenkollen Ski Jump (Holmenkollbakken)
entities.notes: Iconic Olympic ski jump in the Holmenkollen area of Oslo; part of the Holmenkollen National Arena; steel structure with a prominent ramp

details.description: Wide view from the flat forecourt looking up the Holmenkollen ski‑jump arena. The central in‑run and landing hill are clad in dark metal grating, bordered by very broad tiered concrete spectator stands. Above rises the distinctive silver-gray cantilevered steel structure with perforated lattice sides that curve up to the overhanging jump tower and viewing platform. Tall floodlight masts flank the jump, and dense evergreen forest covers the surrounding hillside under an overcast sky. A few visitors walk across the open gravel area at the base near small service buildings.

entities.name: Holmenkollbakken (Holmenkollen Ski Jump)
entities.notes: Iconic cantilevered steel ski‑jump with perforated lattice sides and a viewing platform at the top.

entities.name: Holmenkollen
entities.notes: Hillside area of Oslo that hosts the national ski arena.

details.description: A wide, empty paved plaza occupies the foreground with two pedestrians walking. Behind, the Holmenkollen ski jumping stadium rises into a wooded hillside, featuring a long, steep in-run ramp flanked by concrete seating terraces. A white metal framework arches over the top, and tall floodlights line the sides. Dense evergreen trees form a backdrop under an overcast sky, conveying a chilly outdoor sports complex near Oslo.

entities.name: Holmenkollen ski jump (Holmenkollbakken)
entities.notes: Holmenkollen ski jumping venue with in-run and seating terraces.

details.description: A low-angle, wide shot of the Holmenkollen Ski Jump (Holmenkollbakken) in Oslo, Norway, viewed from the bottom of the landing slope (outrun). The massive structure features a distinctive modern steel truss design that curves upwards into the overcast, grey sky. The landing hill is flanked by steep concrete spectator stands (bleachers) on both sides. Tall, thin floodlight poles rise around the perimeter. In the foreground, a large, flat grey gravel or paved area serves as the outrun, where two people are walking towards the right, providing scale to the immense sporting facility. Dark coniferous trees line the slopes on either side of the jump.

entities.notes: Famous ski jump, rebuilt in 2011, designed by JDS Architects

details.description: A wide-angle, low-angle shot of the Holmenkollbakken ski jump in Oslo, Norway. The structure features a prominent, modern steel lattice cantilever at the top of the jump. The foreground is a large, open, grey paved or gravel area where a few people are walking. Tall floodlight towers are positioned around the site. The background consists of a dense forest of evergreen trees under a heavy, overcast grey sky.

details.description: A large, contemporary ski jumping hill with a distinctive steel-and-aluminum cantilevered tower and lattice side panels rises above stepped grandstand seating. The inrun curves up to an elevated takeoff with a mesh-like guardrail and an observation platform at the top. Long stairways flank the landing hill, and tall floodlight masts stand in the forecourt. Coniferous forest borders the venue. The sky is overcast and the wide gravel/asphalt plaza in the foreground has a few visitors walking.

details.description: The image shows a modern outdoor stadium or arena with a distinctive white curved canopy structure arching over the seating area. The grandstand is composed of multiple tiered concrete steps with light-gray seating, forming a sweeping curve along the slope. In the foreground is a large paved area (asphalt) that appears almost like a plaza or forecourt. A few people are walking across the open space, providing a sense of scale. A tall floodlight mast rises near the seating, and evergreen trees line the back of the scene. The sky is overcast, giving a muted, cool light to the scene. The overall architectural character suggests a contemporary European stadium with a signature roof canopy and integrated hillside setting.

details.description: A modern ski-jumping venue with a prominent steel cantilevered tower and mesh-sided in-run rises above steep concrete terraced seating. The landing slope is flanked by stairways and railings, with tall floodlight masts in a broad gravel/asphalt forecourt at the base. Coniferous forest borders the site on the hillside, and a few pedestrians are visible. The overcast sky gives the scene a muted gray tone. The distinctive sleek, curved profile matches the contemporary Holmenkollen ski jump design.

details.description: Exterior view of a large outdoor sports stadium built into a sloped hillside. In the foreground is a wide paved plaza; behind it sits a long, stepped concrete grandstand that curves upward and meets a white lattice roof canopy supported by slender arches. Tall light towers rise above the seating area, with evergreen trees in the background. The overall palette is grey under an overcast sky, and the architecture emphasizes exposed concrete, large-scale seating blocks, and a high-tech metal framework.

details.description: A low-angle photograph of the Holmenkollen Ski Jump in Oslo, Norway, captured with the image rotated 90 degrees clockwise (sky is on the left, ground on the right). The image features the massive, modern steel lattice structure of the ski jump tower rising steeply against a cloudy grey sky. The in-run is supported by a distinctive cantilevered steel truss design. The landing slope is visible, lined with concrete stairs where several people are walking. The Olympic rings are mounted on the side of the judges' tower or platform beneath the takeoff table, commemorating the site's history as a 1952 Winter Olympics venue. Tall floodlights and dark coniferous trees flank the structure.

entities.notes: Designed by JDS Architects, opened in 2010

details.description: A low-angle shot of the Holmenkollen Ski Jump in Oslo, Norway, under an overcast sky. The image features the iconic curved steel cantilever structure of the jump ramp, which is supported by a white lattice-like framework. The landing slope is visible, flanked by tiered concrete spectator stands. Near the base of the jump, a small building displays the Olympic rings. Tall floodlight towers are positioned around the site, and the background is filled with dense evergreen trees from the surrounding Nordmarka forest. A few people are seen walking on the flat, gravelly outrun area in the foreground.
